column7: 232 severe influenza patients with secondary nosocomial infection described in abstract
column11: Nosocomial infection occurring after admission among severe influenza patients

column7: 1260 hospitalized patients with microbiologically confirmed influenza over 10 seasons; 110 nosocomial cases
column11: Nosocomial influenza defined as symptom onset 72 h after admission
column20: Hospital-acquired vs community-acquired influenza status among confirmed influenza patients
column21: Hospital-acquired influenza was independently associated with greater bacterial superinfection
column32: Comparator is HAI vs CAI among already infected patients, not infected vs non-infected
column33: HAI carried higher burden of secondary bacterial complication
column34: Use HAI status as a secondary-infection escalation modifier

column6: Beijing Ditan Hospital, Capital Medical University
column7: 185 hospitalized VRTI patients with hospital-acquired DRPs infection; 280 drug-resistant isolates
column11: DRPs detected after the first 48 h of hospitalization
column14: DRPs isolates in mixed VRTI patients
column20: Hospital-acquired drug-resistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa in VRTI patients
column21: Pseudomonas aeruginosa was the most frequent overall isolate (46/280), mainly from sputum, with >89% resistance to ceftriaxone and cefotaxime, but relatively higher susceptibility to ceftazidime, cefepime, and amikacin
column32: Mixed influenza/COVID cohort; not influenza-only superinfection
column33: P. aeruginosa was a dominant resistant overlap pathogen with major beta-lactam resistance
column34: Add P. aeruginosa as a major overlap organism in pathogen-interactions logic

column6: Beijing Ditan Hospital, Capital Medical University
column7: 185 hospitalized VRTI patients with hospital-acquired DRPs infection; 280 drug-resistant isolates
column11: DRPs detected after the first 48 h of hospitalization
column14: DRPs isolates in mixed VRTI patients
column20: Hospital-acquired drug-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae in VRTI patients
column21: Klebsiella pneumoniae was the second most frequent isolate (45/280) and showed high resistance to penicillins, cephalosporins, quinolones, and carbapenems, with imipenem resistance 53.3% and meropenem resistance 46.7%
column32: Mixed influenza/COVID cohort; not influenza-only superinfection
column33: K. pneumoniae was a major resistant overlap pathogen with alarming carbapenem resistance
column34: Add K. pneumoniae as a high-priority overlap organism

column6: Beijing Ditan Hospital, Capital Medical University
column7: 185 hospitalized VRTI patients with hospital-acquired DRPs infection; 280 drug-resistant isolates
column11: DRPs detected after the first 48 h of hospitalization
column14: DRPs isolates in mixed VRTI patients
column20: Hospital-acquired drug-resistant Acinetobacter baumannii in VRTI patients
column21: Acinetobacter baumannii was the third most frequent isolate (42/280), predominated in BAL fluid, and was highly resistant to nearly all tested antibiotics, with only partial susceptibility to minocycline and trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole
column32: Mixed influenza/COVID cohort; not influenza-only superinfection
column33: A. baumannii represented a highly resistant overlap organism with very limited treatment options
column34: Add A. baumannii as a critical resistant overlap pathogen

column6: Beijing Ditan Hospital, Capital Medical University
column7: 185 hospitalized VRTI patients with hospital-acquired DRPs infection; 280 drug-resistant isolates
column11: DRPs detected after the first 48 h of hospitalization
column14: DRPs isolates in mixed VRTI patients
column20: Hospital-acquired drug-resistant Escherichia coli in VRTI patients
column21: Escherichia coli was a frequent isolate (26/280), predominated in urine, and showed high resistance to quinolones and third-generation cephalosporins while retaining partial carbapenem and amikacin susceptibility
column32: Mixed influenza/COVID cohort; not influenza-only superinfection
column33: E. coli contributed to resistant overlap, especially in urinary-source specimens
column34: Add E. coli as a urinary-source overlap organism

column6: Beijing Ditan Hospital, Capital Medical University
column7: 185 hospitalized VRTI patients with hospital-acquired DRPs infection; 280 drug-resistant isolates
column11: DRPs detected after the first 48 h of hospitalization
column14: DRPs isolates in mixed VRTI patients
column20: Hospital-acquired drug-resistant Stenotrophomonas maltophilia in VRTI patients
column21: Stenotrophomonas maltophilia was a frequent isolate (25/280) with broad beta-lactam and carbapenem resistance, but preserved susceptibility to minocycline and trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole
column32: Mixed influenza/COVID cohort; not influenza-only superinfection
column33: S. maltophilia was an important resistant overlap pathogen with narrow remaining options
column34: Add S. maltophilia as an overlap organism with distinct treatment constraints

column6: Beijing Ditan Hospital, Capital Medical University
column7: 185 hospitalized VRTI patients with hospital-acquired DRPs infection; 280 drug-resistant isolates
column11: DRPs detected after the first 48 h of hospitalization
column14: DRPs isolates in mixed VRTI patients
column20: Hospital-acquired drug-resistant Enterococcus faecium in VRTI patients
column21: Enterococcus faecium accounted for 16 isolates and showed complete resistance to clindamycin and penicillin, with better susceptibility to linezolid and vancomycin
column32: Mixed influenza/COVID cohort; not influenza-only superinfection
column33: E. faecium was a relevant Gram-positive resistant overlap organism
column34: Add E. faecium as a Gram-positive overlap organism
